abstract: As structured data continues to proliferate across domains such as computational biology, information retrieval, and natural language processing, the need for effective large-scale relational learning has become paramount. In this paper, we introduce a novel neural network architecture designed to embed multi-relational graphs into a versatile continuous vector space. This approach not only preserves the integrity of the original data but actively enhances it by encoding the underlying semantics of the graph to differentiate between plausible and implausible components. Our empirical evaluations demonstrate that this model achieves competitive performance on standard link prediction benchmarks and scales effectively to real-world knowledge bases like WordNet. Furthermore, we illustrate the practical utility of our method in the context of open-text semantic parsing. By applying our framework to word-sense disambiguation, we show that the model can successfully map free-text sentences to structured meaning representations, scaling to environments with tens of thousands of nodes and thousands of unique relation types.
